sentences.target: Wir machen morgen eine Tour durch die Stadt.
sentences.reading: /vɪʁ ˈmaχən ˈmɔʁɡən ˈaɪ̯nə tuːɐ̯ dʊʁç diː ʃtat/

translations.de: Wir machen morgen eine Tour durch die Stadt.
translations.ipa: /vɪʁ ˈmaχən ˈmɔʁɡən ˈaɪ̯nə tuːɐ̯ dʊʁç diː ʃtat/
translations.en: We’re doing a tour through the city tomorrow.
translations.es: Mañana vamos a hacer un recorrido por la ciudad.
translations.fr: Demain, on fait une visite de la ville.
translations.it: Domani facciamo un giro per la città.
translations.ru: Завтра мы пойдём на экскурсию по городу.
translations.pt: Amanhã vamos fazer um passeio pela cidade.
translations.vi: Ngày mai chúng mình sẽ đi tham quan thành phố.
translations.nl: Morgen gaan we de stad verkennen.
translations.uk: Завтра ми підемо на екскурсію містом.
translations.hi: कल हम शहर घूमने जा रहे हैं.
translations.el: Αύριο θα κάνουμε μια βόλτα στην πόλη.
translations.nb: I morgen skal vi ta en tur i byen.
translations.id: Besok kita ikut tur keliling kota.
translations.sv: I morgon ska vi göra en rundtur i stan.
translations.ro: Mâine facem un tur al orașului.
translations.da: I morgen tager vi på en rundtur i byen.
translations.ja: 明日、市内観光に行きます。

tokens.explanation: Personal pronoun; 1st person plural nominative; subject of the finite verb.
Main lexical verb; finite, present indicative, 1st person plural; heads the predicate and selects the object.
Temporal adverb modifying the clause; indicates time of the action.
Indefinite article; feminine accusative singular; determiner of ‘Tour’.
Common noun; feminine accusative singular; direct object of ‘machen’.
Preposition governing accusative; introduces a prepositional phrase expressing path.
Definite article; feminine accusative singular; determiner of ‘Stadt’ required by ‘durch’.
Common noun; feminine accusative singular; object of the preposition ‘durch’.

sentences.target: Die Band startet heute ihre Tour.

translations.de: Die Band startet heute ihre Tour.
translations.en: The band starts their tour today.
translations.es: La banda empieza su gira hoy.
translations.fr: Le groupe commence sa tournée aujourd’hui.
translations.it: La band inizia il tour oggi.
translations.yue: 樂隊今日開始巡迴演出。
translations.pt: A banda começa a turnê hoje.
translations.he: הלהקה מתחילה היום את סיבוב ההופעות.
translations.vi: Ban nhạc bắt đầu chuyến lưu diễn hôm nay.
translations.nl: De band begint vandaag aan hun tour.
translations.hu: A zenekar ma kezdi a turnéját.
translations.hi: बैंड आज अपना टूर शुरू कर रहा है.
translations.el: Το συγκρότημα ξεκινά σήμερα την περιοδεία του.
translations.nb: Bandet starter turneen sin i dag.
translations.sv: Bandet startar sin turné i dag.
translations.da: Bandet starter deres turné i dag.
translations.ja: バンドは今日からツアーを始めます。

tokens.explanation: Definite article; feminine nominative singular; determiner of ‘Band’.
Common noun; feminine nominative singular; subject of the clause (music group).
Main lexical verb; finite, present indicative, 3rd person singular; predicate of the sentence.
Temporal adverb modifying the verb; indicates time.
Possessive determiner; agrees with feminine accusative singular ‘Tour’; possessor is the (feminine) subject ‘Band’.
Common noun; feminine accusative singular; direct object of ‘startet’ (what is being started).

sentences.target: Du kannst die Tour online buchen.

translations.de: Du kannst die Tour online buchen.
translations.en: You can book the tour online.
translations.es: Puedes reservar el tour en línea.
translations.fr: Tu peux réserver la visite en ligne.
translations.zh-Hans: 你可以在网上预订这次行程。
translations.yue: 你可以喺網上預訂個團。
translations.pt: Você pode reservar o tour online.
translations.vi: Bạn có thể đặt tour trực tuyến.
translations.nl: Je kunt de tour online boeken.
translations.hi: तुम यह टूर ऑनलाइन बुक कर सकते हो.
translations.el: Μπορείς να κλείσεις την ξενάγηση διαδικτυακά.
translations.nb: Du kan bestille turen på nett.
translations.id: Kamu bisa pesan tur secara online.
translations.ja: ツアーはオンラインで予約できます。

tokens.explanation: Personal pronoun; 2nd person singular nominative; subject.
Modal auxiliary; finite, present indicative, 2nd person singular; forms a verbal complex with the infinitive ‘buchen’.
Definite article; feminine accusative singular; determiner of ‘Tour’.
Common noun; feminine accusative singular; direct object selected by ‘buchen’.
Adverb of manner/medium; modifies ‘buchen’.
Main lexical verb; infinitive complement of the modal ‘kannst’; heads the object ‘die Tour’.

sentences.target: Ich freue mich auf die Tour morgen.
sentences.reading: /ɪç ˈfʁɔʏ̯ə mɪç aʊ̯f diː tuːɐ̯ ˈmɔʁɡən/

translations.de: Ich freue mich auf die Tour morgen.
translations.ipa: /ɪç ˈfʁɔʏ̯ə mɪç aʊ̯f diː tuːɐ̯ ˈmɔʁɡən/
translations.en: I’m looking forward to the tour tomorrow.
translations.es: Tengo muchas ganas del tour de mañana.
translations.fr: J’ai hâte de faire la visite demain.
translations.it: Non vedo l’ora di fare il tour domani.
translations.pt: Estou animado para o tour de amanhã.
translations.vi: Mình đang mong chờ tour ngày mai.
translations.nl: Ik kijk uit naar de tour van morgen.
translations.uk: Я з нетерпінням чекаю на завтрашній тур.
translations.hu: Már alig várom a holnapi túrát.
translations.hi: मुझे कल के टूर का इंतज़ार है.
translations.pl: Nie mogę się doczekać jutrzejszej wycieczki.
translations.nb: Jeg gleder meg til turen i morgen.
translations.id: Aku tak sabar menantikan tur besok.
translations.sv: Jag ser fram emot turen i morgon.
translations.cs: Už se těším na zítřejší prohlídku.
translations.da: Jeg glæder mig til turen i morgen.
translations.ja: 明日のツアーが楽しみです。

tokens.explanation: Personal pronoun; 1st person singular nominative; subject.
Main lexical verb; finite, present indicative, 1st person singular; part of the pronominal construction ‘sich freuen (auf)’.
Reflexive personal pronoun; accusative 1st person singular; required reflexive object of ‘freuen’.
Preposition (governing accusative here); selected by ‘sich freuen’ to mark what one looks forward to (prepositional object).
Definite article; feminine accusative singular; determiner of ‘Tour’.
Common noun; feminine accusative singular; object of the preposition ‘auf’ (prepositional object).
Temporal adverb modifying the clause; indicates when the tour is (looked forward to).

Die Band startet heute ihre Tour.
Du kannst die Tour online buchen.
Ich freue mich auf die Tour morgen.

en: We’re doing a tour through the city tomorrow.
The band starts their tour today.
You can book the tour online.
I’m looking forward to the tour tomorrow.
